QF.id: VB Legal Structure of Mutual Funds_13

question.text: Which of the following must a person obtain before they can distribute (sell) mutual funds?

answer.text: A valid NISM certification and an ARN (AMFI Registration Number)
Only a college degree in commerce or finance
A license from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** To distribute mutual funds in India, a person must: (1) Pass the relevant NISM certification exam (Series V-A for regular distributors, Series V-B for new cadre distributors), and (2) Register with AMFI to obtain an ARN (AMFI Registration Number). Without both of these, you cannot legally sell mutual funds. **The Trap:** A commerce degree alone is not sufficient. RBI licenses are for banking activities. Stock exchange membership is for stockbrokers. Mutual fund distribution requires NISM + ARN specifically. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under distributor requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** You are appearing for the NISM Series V-B exam right now — once you pass, register with AMFI for your ARN. Keep your certification current (it needs renewal) to maintain your ARN and continue distributing.
